skill.name: installing-skill-tracker
skill.description: Installs Claude Code project hooks that record skill usage events, verification results, and user prompts in local activity logs for later analysis.
skill.summary: Install local Claude Code hooks to measure skill usage and verification outcomes.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static command and weak-crypto findings are mostly false positives from documented shell commands, bash variable substitution, and benign names such as used_skills. The confirmed risks are local full-prompt logging, executable hook installation, and persistent changes to .claude/settings.json. No evidence found of network exfiltration, credential theft, malicious prompt injection, or actual weak cryptography.

medium_findings.title: Full user prompts are logged locally
medium_findings.description: The installed prompt hook reads the hook input, extracts the prompt, and appends it to .claude/activity-logs/prompts.jsonl. This is useful for analytics, but it can persist secrets, private business data, or regulated content entered in prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hook script explicitly extracts .prompt and writes it to a local JSONL file. The behavior is intentional and directly supported by the cited lines.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Claude Code hooks are installed
medium_findings.description: The setup script writes executable hook scripts and registers them for prompt submission, read or bash pre-tool use, and bash post-tool use. The commands are fixed local paths, but persistent hooks can observe future project activity until removed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script defines hook configuration, writes hook files, marks them executable, and updates settings.json. The commands are not dynamic, which lowers severity.

low_findings.title: Static external command findings are mostly benign shell usage
low_findings.description: The flagged command substitutions read hook JSON input, call jq, call date, and document setup or verification commands. I did not find evidence that prompt text, file paths, or command strings are executed with eval or an unsafely constructed shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited shell snippets use jq --arg and regex matching rather than evaluating captured values. Documentation command blocks are expected for an installation skill.

low_findings.title: High static crypto and certificate findings appear false positive
low_findings.description: The flagged SKILL.md line is frontmatter description text, not cryptographic code. The flagged setup.py line refers to a used_skills variable in an analysis script, not a certificate, key file, or secret material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain metadata and ordinary analytics variable names. No weak algorithm, certificate file, private key, or secret handling appears there.

low_findings.title: Verification script performs local environment checks
low_findings.description: The verification script checks local directories, executable bits, jq availability, and settings JSON validity. This is system reconnaissance in a narrow setup-validation context and does not collect or transmit results externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code only checks local installation state and prints results. No evidence found of network access or broad host enumeration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local prompt capture hook
dangerous_patterns.description: A UserPromptSubmit hook captures full prompt text and stores it in a project-local JSONL log.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script extracts the prompt field and appends it to prompts.jsonl using jq. This is confirmed by direct source context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable hook scripts and settings modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer creates executable hook scripts and modifies .claude/settings.json to run those scripts on future events.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The setup flow writes files, changes permissions, and persists hook configuration. The command paths are static and local.

content.value_statement: Teams need visibility into which Claude Code skills are used and whether procedural skills succeed. This skill installs local hooks and analysis scripts that collect usage events inside the project.
actual_capabilities: Creates .claude/hooks and .claude/activity-logs directories in the current project.
Installs hook scripts that record prompt submissions and skill activation events.
Adds UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, and PostToolUse hook entries to .claude/settings.json.
Installs an analysis script that summarizes invocations, success rates, failures, and unused skills.
Provides a verification script that checks directories, hook scripts, jq, and settings configuration.
limitations: Requires jq to be installed on the local machine.
Stores full user prompts in local project logs, which may include sensitive information.
Tracks only events visible through the configured Claude Code hooks.
Does not send data to a dashboard or remote analytics service.

use_cases.title: Measure skill adoption in a project
use_cases.description: Install local tracking so a team can see which skills are used during Claude Code sessions.

use_cases.title: Review procedural skill reliability
use_cases.description: Analyze verification outcomes to find skills with repeated failures or missing verification events.

use_cases.title: Prepare local usage reports
use_cases.description: Generate a local summary of prompt activity, skill invocations, and unused installed skills.

prompt_templates.title: Install tracking
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup in a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the skill tracker hooks in this project and tell me whether setup completed successfully.

prompt_templates.title: Verify installation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the skill tracker verification and summarize any missing hook, directory, or dependency.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze skill usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Review collected activity after several sessions
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the collected skill usage logs and summarize invocations, success rates, and unused skills.

prompt_templates.title: Audit tracker impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the installed tracker hooks and explain what data is logged, where it is stored, and how to disable it.

output_examples.output: The report states whether required hook scripts exist, whether they are executable, whether jq is installed, and whether settings contain the expected hooks.

output_examples.input: Analyze skill usage for this project.
output_examples.output: The summary lists procedural skills, content skills, invocation counts, verification success rates, high failure rates, and unused skills.
The response identifies prompt logs, skill start events, verification outcomes, session identifiers, timestamps, and local storage paths.

best_practices: Review prompt logging policies before installing the hooks in repositories with sensitive work.
Run verification after setup and after any manual change to .claude/settings.json.
Rotate or delete local activity logs when they are no longer needed.
anti_patterns: Do not install tracking in projects where full prompts must not be stored locally.
Do not publish activity logs without reviewing them for secrets or private content.
Do not edit hook commands manually unless you understand Claude Code hook behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill install?
faq.answer: It installs local hook scripts, an analysis script, activity log directories, and Claude Code settings entries.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files store data locally and do not show network transmission.

faq.question: What data is logged?
faq.answer: It logs full prompts, session identifiers, timestamps, skill names, and verification status events.

faq.question: Can it track skills that do not have verify.py?
faq.answer: Yes. The analysis script separates content skills from procedural skills with verification scripts.

faq.question: What dependency is required?
faq.answer: The installed shell hooks require jq for JSON parsing and log creation.

faq.question: How can tracking be disabled?
faq.answer: Remove the installed hook entries from .claude/settings.json and delete the generated hook scripts if desired.
